perturbation.original_text: 1.21 "Current Good Manufacturing Practices" or "cGMP" means all applicable standards and applicable Laws (as defined below) relating to manufacturing practices for products (including ingredients, testing, storage, handling, intermediates, bulk and finished products) promulgated by the FDA or any other applicable Governmental Authority (including, without limitation, EU or member state level) having jurisdiction, including, but not limited to, standards in the form of applicable Laws, guidelines, advisory opinions and compliance policy guides and current interpretations of the applicable authority or agency thereof (as applicable to pharmaceutical and biological products and ingredients), as the same may be updated, supplemented or amended from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.21 "Current Good Manufacturing Practices" or "cGMP" means the minimum applicable standards as interpreted and applied solely by the Manufacturer, for manufacturing practices for products (including ingredients, testing, storage, handling, intermediates, bulk and finished products). These standards are based on the Manufacturer's internal guidelines and may not align with those set by the FDA or any other Governmental Authority.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by defining cGMP as the Manufacturer's interpretation of minimum standards, which conflicts with the original text where cGMP refers to all applicable standards and applicable Laws relating to manufacturing practices as promulgated by the FDA or any other applicable Governmental Authority, which also is in conflict with section 4.2 Manufacturing standards, where the Manufacturer shall manufacture and supply each Product in accordance with applicable laws. This could lead to uncertainty on whether Manufacturer adheres to recognized and regulated standards or if it is enough to apply internal minimum standards.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.2 Manufacturing Standards. Manufacturer shall manufacture and supply each Product (including disposing of all Waste and other materials) in accordance with all applicable Specifications, applicable Laws, requirements under the applicable Quality Agreement, and this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Current Good Manufacturing Practices" (cGMP) as the Manufacturer's internal interpretation of minimum standards, explicitly stating that these standards "may not align with those set by the FDA or any other Governmental Authority." This directly contradicts section 4.2, which mandates that the Manufacturer must manufacture and supply each Product in accordance with "all applicable Specifications, applicable Laws... and this Agreement." By allowing the Manufacturer to deviate from FDA or other governmental authority standards, the modified text creates a contradiction, as the Manufacturer could potentially be in compliance with its internal 'minimum' standards while simultaneously violating applicable Laws and specifications as required by section 4.2. The change directly alters the definition of a critical term leading to a contradiction, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 Customer's Intellectual Property. Customer hereby grants to Manufacturer a non-exclusive license during the Term to use any Customer Property and Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments solely in connection with Manufacturer performing its obligations under this Agreement or the Facility Addendum in accordance with the terms hereof or thereof, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 Customer's Intellectual Property. Customer hereby grants to Manufacturer an exclusive license during the Term to use any Customer Property and Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments solely in connection with Manufacturer performing its obligations under this Agreement or the Facility Addendum in accordance with the terms hereof or thereof, as applicable. This ensures no other party can use this intellectual property for similar purposes within the scope of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The changed text now grants an exclusive license instead of a non-exclusive license. This change conflicts with Section 8.2 (b), where the intelectual property, and Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments, will be the exclusive property of the Customer, and the manufacturer transfers all rights, title and interest in and to each of the Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments to Customer free and clear of any encumbrances. The contradiction arises because if the licenge is exclusive, the Manufacturer cannot assign it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Manufacturer acknowledges and agrees that, as between the Parties, any Improvements or Developments that are specific to and otherwise solely relate to, the manufacturing, processing or packaging of Products (such Improvements and Developments, collectively, "Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments") shall be the exclusive property of Customer, and Customer shall own all rights, title and interest in and to such Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments. Manufacturer agrees to and hereby does irrevocably transfer, assign and convey, and shall cause its Personnel to irrevocably transfer, assign and convey, all rights, title and interest in and to each of the Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments to Customer free and clear of any encumbrances, and Manufacturer agrees to execute, and shall cause its subcontractors and Personnel to execute, all documents necessary to do so. All such assignments shall include existing or prospective Intellectual Property rights therein in any country.
perturbation.justification: The change from a non-exclusive to an exclusive license in Section 8.1 directly contradicts Section 8.2(b). Section 8.2(b) states that the Customer-Owned Improvements and Developments shall be the exclusive property of the Customer and the Manufacturer transfers all rights, title, and interest to the Customer. If the Manufacturer grants an exclusive license to use the intellectual property, as stated in the changed Section 8.1, it cannot simultaneously transfer all rights, title, and interest to the Customer in Section 8.2(b), because an exclusive license implies some retained rights or control. This creates a clear and irreconcilable conflict, making the contract unenforceable. The explanation provided in the prompt also highlights the contradiction in that 'if the license is exclusive, the Manufacturer cannot assign it.'

perturbation.original_text: 10.4 Limitations on Liability. (a) Except in the event of (i) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.1, (ii) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.2, (iii) the gross negligence, fraud or willful misconduct of a Party or its Personnel, (iv) a Party's willful breach of this Agreement, (v) a breach of Section 13 or (vi) customer liabilities pursuant to, and subject to the limitations set forth in, Section 2.5(e), neither Party's aggregate liability to the other Party (or its Personnel that are indemnitees under Section 10.1 or Section 10.2, as applicable) under this Agreement for the initial twelve (12) month period immediately following the Effective Date, and for any twelve (12) month period thereafter during the Term, shall exceed, on a cumulative basis, the amount that is one and one half (11∕2) times the aggregate amounts paid or payable pursuant to this Agreement in the preceding twelve (12) month period preceding the loss date by Customer to Manufacturer but solely with respect to the supply hereunder of Product (or Products) for which such corresponding liability arose (the "Affected Products") and not any other Products (or if, as of the time the liability arises, this Agreement has not been in effect for twelve (12) months, then the amounts paid or payable by Customer to Manufacturer hereunder during the period from the Effective Date until such time the liability arises, shall be annualized to a full twelve (12) months but solely with respect to the supply hereunder of the Affected Product(s) and not any other Products).
perturbation.changed_text: 10.4 Limitations on Liability. (a) Except in the event of (i) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.1, (ii) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.2, (iii) the gross negligence, fraud or willful misconduct of a Party or its Personnel, (iv) a Party's willful breach of this Agreement, (v) a breach of Section 13 or (vi) customer liabilities pursuant to, and subject to the limitations set forth in, Section 2.5(e), neither Party's aggregate liability to the other Party (or its Personnel that are indemnitees under Section 10.1 or Section 10.2, as applicable) under this Agreement is unlimited and shall extend to all claims, losses, and damages incurred under this agreement. This applies without regard to the initial twelve (12) month period immediately following the Effective Date, and for any twelve (12) month period thereafter during the Term, so there shall not be any limitation or exceeding of the amount that is one and one half (11∕2) times the aggregate amounts paid or payable pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change eliminates the limitation on aggregate liability, but it mentions one and one half (11∕2) times the aggregate amounts paid or payable pursuant to this Agreement, which is contradictory, and creates uncertainty about what the totaly amount liable should be. Does it apply a limit or does it extends to all clams, losses, and damages?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.4 Limitations on Liability. (a) Except in the event of (i) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.1, (ii) Third Party Claims subject to a Party's indemnification obligations pursuant to Section 10.2, (iii) the gross negligence, fraud or willful misconduct of a Party or its Personnel, (iv) a Party's willful breach of this Agreement, (v) a breach of Section 13 or (vi) customer liabilities pursuant to, and subject to the limitations set forth in, Section 2.5(e), neither Party's aggregate liability to the other Party (or its Personnel that are indemnitees under Section 10.1 or Section 10.2, as applicable) under this Agreement is unlimited and shall extend to all claims, losses, and damages incurred under this agreement. This applies without regard to the initial twelve (12) month period immediately following the Effective Date, and for any twelve (12) month period thereafter during the Term, so there shall not be any limitation or exceeding of the amount that is one and one half (11∕2) times the aggregate amounts paid or payable pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text eliminates the original limitation on aggregate liability but simultaneously refers to "one and one half (11∕2) times the aggregate amounts paid or payable pursuant to this Agreement." This creates a direct conflict because the phrase implies a limitation based on a calculation, contradicting the statement that liability is now unlimited. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, now contains this internal inconsistency, making it unclear whether liability is capped or not, thus presenting a strong in-text contradiction.
